title: Smoky Crispy Peruvian Tomato Salad
description: A smoky, crispy tomato salad with quinoa, avocado, and Peruvian achi amarillo, perfect for meal prep. Its burst of smoky flavor and crisp textures make it a Mediterranean-friendly lunch.
instructions: Fill a large pot with water, bring to a rolling boil. Add a pinch of salt.
While water heats, wash the plum tomatoes. When the water is boiling, add the tomatoes and blanch for 30 seconds.
Remove the tomatoes with a slotted spoon and transfer them immediately to a bowl of ice water to stop cooking. Let them sit for 1 minute.
Drain the tomatoes, pat dry with paper towels, then peel the skins—they should slide off easily.
Heat the olive oil in a non‑stick skillet over medium‑high heat. Add the peeled tomatoes in a single layer, spicy side down, and let them sear for 3–4 minutes until edges start to blister.
Sprinkle the smoked paprika, achi amarillo paste, salt, and black pepper over the tomatoes. Cook for another 2 minutes, stirring gently, until the tomatoes are crispy and aromatic.
Remove the skillet from the heat and allow the tomatoes to cool slightly.
In a large bowl, combine the cooled tomatoes, cooked quinoa, diced avocado, crumbled feta, chopped cilantro, and lime juice. Toss gently to mix.
Taste and adjust seasoning with extra salt or lime juice if needed.
Divide the salad into 4 meal‑prep containers, cover, and refrigerate. Serve chilled or at room temperature.
